module.name: ValueSetter
module.param: name productName department description className subclassName devicePool mailId userFullName userId callingSearchSpaceName protocol securityProfileName directoryNumber clusterId lastUpdated

module.name: HTML
module.param:
The app contains a "devices lookup" through which it is possible to extend your data with many useful device-level fields. This can be completed either manually or automatically on a schedule.

These device-level fields include: name, productName, department, description, className, subclassName, devicePool, mailId, userFullName, userId, callingSearchSpaceName, protocol, securityProfileName, directoryNumber

What is the end result?

  1. In Investigate > Calls these fields will all be avaiable for filtering and reporting, prefixed with "orig" or "dest" as appropriate.
  2. In Investigate Devices you will also be able to use "Edit Fields" to add any of these fields to your tabular reports and/or to use these fields and values as search filters.
  3. Also in Investigate Devices you will be able to pick the special option "include devices with zero calls" to create reports that spell out which devices are totally unused and can be removed from CallManager.

How do you go about this?

  • Manually: Download the empty csv using the "Download CSV" tab here. add the rows and values in Excel. Then save it as a raw CSV file and upload it using the "Upload CSV" tab here.
  • Automatically, with our AXL app: Sideview's Supporting App for AXL can connect to your publishers directly via AXL and pull all of the information across, every night or on a schedule you define. This app is not free, but it does have a 60 day free trial. Contact us to find out more.
  • Automatically, as implemented by Splunk admins/consultants or experts. This is more rare but if your team has the resources this is an option. Ultimately the end result is something populating the devices CSV-lookup in Splunk with the right values. Note although we do not offer this as a service we are happy to advise.

Remember to take backups

By default nothing in the app and nothing in Splunk will be preserving backups of this information, so make sure to take appropriate backup copies yourself.

module.name: HTML
module.param: To download a copy of the devices lookup as a csv file or to open it in Excel just click the icon below:

module.name: HTML
module.param:
Upload a new csv here if you wish to replace the devices.csv currently loaded in Splunk. First use the "Download CSV" tab to get the existing csv file because this will ensure you have the correct columns.





NOTES

  1. If you are managing updates to the devices lookup manually, then we strongly recommend saving a copy of this file somewhere as a backup.
  2. If in the future you would like to automate this process to pull of the values nightly from your publishers, read about our Supporting App for AXL.
